default.summary: Water availability and cost vary by basin and utility. We recommend WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas and local utility data for site-level assessment.

CA.summary: Variable by region; drought and allocation history. Municipal and industrial supply and cost vary. Check basin and utility; consider reclaimed water for electrolysis.

TX.summary: Gulf and inland basins; industrial water use established. Availability and cost vary by region. Confirm supply and permits for large withdrawals.

NM.summary: Arid; water rights and basin allocation critical. Secure supply and permits early. Reuse and alternative sources may be relevant.

AZ.summary: Water-stressed; Colorado River and groundwater. Industrial users face allocation and cost pressure. Verify supply and long-term rights.

NV.summary: Limited water; allocation tightly managed. Industrial supply and cost vary. Confirm availability and permits for project scale.

WA.summary: Generally adequate supply in many basins; permits and rights still required. Regional variation; check local water rights.

OR.summary: Variable by basin; western Oregon typically more available. Verify rights and cost for industrial use.

WY.summary: Headwaters state; some basins under pressure. Water rights and allocation rules apply. Confirm supply for project.

UT.summary: Great Salt Lake and Colorado River concerns; allocation varies. Secure water rights and assess long-term availability.

CO.summary: Prior appropriation; competition in Front Range and Western Slope. Industrial water and reuse options; verify supply.

IA.summary: Generally adequate surface and groundwater in many areas. Permits and allocation apply. Confirm for large withdrawals.

OK.summary: Variable by region; some groundwater and surface supply. Check basin and permit requirements for industrial use.

KS.summary: Ogallala and surface water; allocation and decline in some areas. Verify availability and cost for project scale.

IL.summary: Great Lakes and Mississippi; generally adequate in many areas. Permits and withdrawals rules apply.

NY.summary: Generally adequate in many regions; permits and watershed rules apply. Confirm for industrial scale.

PA.summary: Ohio River and watersheds; supply typically adequate. Permits and withdrawal limits apply.

OH.summary: Lake Erie and Ohio River; industrial water use established. Verify permits and supply for scale.

FL.summary: Aquifer and surface water; allocation and quality vary. Confirm supply and permits for industrial use.

LA.summary: Mississippi and Gulf; industrial water use common. Supply generally available; permits and discharge rules apply.

ND.summary: Missouri River and groundwater; generally adequate in many areas. Confirm for large withdrawals.

NE.summary: Platte and Ogallala; allocation varies. Verify supply and rights for project scale.

MT.summary: Headwaters and basins; rights and allocation apply. Generally more available in some regions; verify locally.
